title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Poirot and Carter debate the linguistic interpretation of a sentence regarding a woman being "old enough to be his mother." Poirot argues that the word "now" implies a current state, while Carter suggests it refers to a change in a man's perception over time. Poirot clarifies the context of a previous argument between Samuel Kid, who was pretending to be Richard Nigges, and Nancy Duane, who was pretending to be Harriet Sipper. The argument centered on the idea that when a person dies, their age is fixed; therefore, if George Sipper died as a young man, his soul would remain that age, making his widow—who has aged—"old enough to be his mother" in a spiritual sense.

Poirot then reveals the mechanics of the murders at the Bluxton Hotel. He explains that Richard Nigges lured Ada Gransberry to the hotel by promising a reconciliation after sixteen years, which allowed the conspirators to prepare. Poirot concludes that Jane Hobbs killed Harriet Sipper and Richard Nigges killed Ada Gransberry, likely by slipping poison into the water they drank.

Jane confirms the conspiracy, stating that Richard Nigges was tormented by his past actions involving Patrick and Frances Ives. Seeking atonement, Nigges proposed a plan where they would all die to pay for their sins, acting as an "executor" of justice. While Jane agreed that Harriet and Ada deserved to die, she did not want to die herself. However, she felt compelled to comply because Nigges had become a paranoid madman who would have killed her if she refused.
